title: Transaction
description: Base entity for financial transactions. For monetary amounts, Plaid expects a decimal amount, with two places to represent fractional values of the base currency, for example `101.99`

transactionId.description: Long term persistent identity of the transaction (unique to account). Plaid expects that `status: PENDING` and `status: POSTED` transactions have different IDs

referenceTransactionId.description:
For reverse postings, the identity of the transaction being
reversed. For the correction transaction, the identity of the
reversing post. For credit card posting transactions, the identity
of the authorization transaction

postedTimestamp.description:
The date and time that the transaction was posted to the account.
This property is **required** by Plaid when `status=POSTED`. Plaid expects this property to be omitted when `status=PENDING`
ISO 8601 date-time in format `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.nnn[Z|[+|-]hh:mm]` according to
[IETF RFC 3339](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)

transactionTimestamp.description:
The date and time that the transaction was added to the server backend systems
ISO 8601 date-time in format `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.nnn[Z|[+|-]hh:mm]` according to
[IETF RFC 3339](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3339#section-5.6)

cardNumberDisplay.description:
The payment card number (e.g. debit, credit or digital), suitably masked,
used to originate the transaction. May differ from primary account number as a
secondary or employee card or a one-time use number. This is an optional field
and won't be returned for certain types of transactions such as cash or check deposits

description.description:
Description of the transaction, such as information about a merchant's
name or place of business in a manner that is user friendly and accessible to the customer

category.description: Transaction category, preferably MCC or SIC. Plaid expects your organization to provide MCC, if available and applicable

subCategory.description:
Transaction category detail specifying the standard of the transaction category.
For example, "MCC"

amount.description: The amount of money in the account currency. The amount is an absolute value. Plaid relies on the `DebitCreditMemo` enum to determine the direction (and sign) of the transaction

foreignAmount.description: The amount of money in the foreign currency. If this amount is specified, then Plaid expects that the `foreignCurrency` property is also set
